questions.question: Why does Vue warn when a child component mutates a prop directly?
options: Props are frozen objects that cannot be mutated for performance reasons
The parent doesn't know about it and will overwrite the change on next re-render
Direct mutation bypasses Vue's reactivity system entirely
It violates the component lifecycle by triggering updates out of order

questions.question: What is the cleanest Vue 3.4+ approach for a component that needs to read and write a single value from the parent?
options: Define the prop with required: false and emit events manually
Use defineModel() to create an automatic two-way binding
Use v-model:value with explicit prop and emit declarations
Copy the prop to a local ref and watch for changes

questions.question: When is it acceptable to copy a prop to local state with ref(props.initialCount)?
options: When the child and parent need to stay in sync at all times
When using the Composition API instead of the Options API
When the prop is truly an initial seed and doesn't need to stay in sync
When the prop is a primitive type like number or string
